title: [nlir][onboarding] checkout refresh persistently fails on ms-mac ‚Äî git fetch 'No user exists for uid 501', beads stale
description:
## Symptom (routed by log-monitor-msm -> ctrl, 2026-07-03)

nlir (newly onboarded ~13:53 today) has a PERSISTENTLY failing checkout refresh on ms-mac: `POST /api/v1/projects/nlir/checkout/refresh -> 500` (fatal=false) recurring every sweep, keeping nlir's beads STALE (board shows nlir as one of the stale projects).

## Root cause (from ms-mac daemon.log)

    checkout refresh error for nlir (consecutive failures: 40): daemon error: git fetch origin main failed: No user exists for uid 501

40+ consecutive failures. The underlying `git fetch origin main` for nlir's checkout fails with "No user exists for uid 501" (getpwuid(501) failing ‚Äî uid 501 = the macOS operator user).

## Key discriminator

This is nlir-SPECIFIC: cacophony's checkout refresh on the SAME ms-mac daemon (pid 81113, healthy, ~6h uptime) works fine. So it is NOT a general daemon-env failure ‚Äî something about nlir's checkout/remote differs.

## NOT the bd-95abff class

This is a git-fetch-ENV error (uid resolution during git fetch), not the bd-95abff silent-materialization-wedge class. Different root; do not fold.

## Fix directions

1. Compare nlir's remote/checkout config vs cacophony's ‚Äî likely nlir uses a remote type (e.g. SSH) or a git config (core.sshCommand / a credential helper) that triggers a getpwuid(501) lookup the daemon's launchd git-subprocess env can't satisfy, whereas cacophony's remote doesn't.
2. If it's a daemon git-subprocess env gap (missing HOME/USER for the fetch), `services.caco-daemon-extra-env` is inherited by the daemon's child git/subprocess operations (per AGENTS.md) so HOME/USER could be set there ‚Äî but confirm WHY cacophony is unaffected first before a blanket env change.
3. This is a NEW-PROJECT ONBOARDING settling issue ‚Äî the nlir owner / config-onboarder (Harry onboarded it ~13:53) should verify nlir's remote + checkout config.

Non-fatal, nlir-specific, ms-mac daemon otherwise healthy. Routed by log-monitor-msm; ctrl found the root cause + filed. cc Harry (nlir onboarder).

## Diagnostic (msm-0, read-only, 2026-07-03 ~19:40)

Refutes fix-direction #1 and narrows to a daemon env bug:

- **Both nlir AND cacophony use `ssh://git@github.com/...` remotes** (checked `caco config show --section projects`). Not a remote-type difference.
- **Both daemon canonical checkouts (`~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/{nlir,cacophony}`) have IDENTICAL local git config**: same `core.sshCommand = ssh -F /dev/null -i ~/.ssh/caco -o IdentitiesOnly=yes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new`, same `remote.origin.url` class, and BOTH have local `user.name`/`user.email` set. So the failure is NOT a checkout-config difference.
- **nlir's checkout is proven functional**: `git -C ~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/nlir ls-remote origin main` (HOME set, normal env) returns the ref cleanly. The key/remote/config all work.
- The `No user exists for uid 501` is git's `getpwuid(501)` failing to resolve HOME when HOME is unset in the fetch subprocess. Since cacophony succeeds on the SAME daemon with IDENTICAL config, this is a **daemon env-propagation bug**: the git-fetch subprocess for the newly-onboarded nlir project is missing HOME (existing projects like cacophony inherit it; a project onboarded mid-daemon-run at ~13:53 apparently does not).

### Recommended fix (daemon/operator lane ‚Äî NOT nlir checkout)
1. Simplest: **restart the ms-mac caco-daemon** so nlir's fetch subprocess inherits HOME like cacophony's (likely clears the 40-consec-failure state, since onboarding mid-run didn't propagate env).
2. If it persists after restart: set `HOME=/Users/harryaskham` (and `USER`) in `services.caco-daemon-extra-env` so every daemon git subprocess has it, or fix the onboarding path to propagate the daemon's env to per-project fetch subprocesses.

nlir's checkout/remote/config need no change. Handing to Harry/ctrl (daemon owners).
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:ms-mac-cacophony-caco-ctrl

title: clippy collapsible_match warning in config.rs env-interpolation
description:
`cargo clippy --lib --tests` on main emits one `collapsible_match` warning at
`src/config.rs:632` (the OS-env interpolation walk from bd-7b1dd4): an inner
`if s.contains('$')` inside a `match serde_yaml::Value::String(s)` arm that
clippy suggests collapsing into a match guard `String(s) if s.contains('$')`.
Pre-existing on main (not a regression); harmless, but it will make a future
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` CI gate (bd-baba99) red until fixed. One-line
change. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-700306 session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-2

title: Decide where MessageViews SPEC defaults live (config resolve vs messages fallback)
description: SPEC ¬ßExample config defines canonical `context.messages.views` defaults (`^`=[assistant], `^_`=[user], `^*`=[user,assistant,system], `^/`=[system]), but config `MessageViews::default()` is all-empty and `resolve_defaults` (src/config.rs) does not populate them. So the built-in `^` behaviour (and the SPEC `msg` test `^-1`) only works because src/messages.rs `effective_roles()` bakes those SPEC defaults as a fallback for an empty configured view. That's a reasonable seam, but it splits "view defaults" across two modules. Decision for the config owner: either (a) centralize the SPEC view defaults in config defaults-resolution (then messages could drop its fallback), or (b) keep them in the messages layer and document config `MessageViews::default()` as intentionally empty. Either is fine; flagging so the two don't silently diverge. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-f9809a/bd-e8064e/bd-43ac5e session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: Shared temp-file test helper to dedup temp_dir()+pid+nanos idiom
description: Context/config unit tests each re-implement the same throwaway-temp-file idiom: `std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("nlir-<tag>-{}-{nanos}", std::process::id()))` + manual `fs::remove_file` cleanup (see src/config.rs tests and the new src/context.rs tests, ~6 call-sites). A tiny shared test helper (e.g. a `TempFile` RAII guard, or a `test_util` module behind `#[cfg(test)]`) would dedup the boilerplate, guarantee cleanup on panic, and avoid pid/nanos-collision copy-paste drift. No `tempfile` dev-dep is present; a ~20-line in-tree helper avoids adding one. Low priority long-tail polish. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-0909ab/bd-fdd3bc session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: Wire double-quote $name interpolation into eval (lexer must carry the quote kind)
description:
SPEC ¬ßInterpolation: a double-quoted string `"‚Ä¶$name‚Ä¶"` interpolates bare `$name` at eval time; single-quoted `'‚Ä¶'` (raw) and bare literals do not. The interpolation FUNCTION exists (context::interpolate, bd-22fa7e), but it is NOT wired into the evaluator, so `nlir -e 'k=x;"v is $k"'` yields the literal "v is $k" instead of "v is x".

Root cause: the lexer collapses both `"‚Ä¶"` and `'‚Ä¶'` into a single `Token::Quoted(String)` with no flag distinguishing interpolating (double) from raw (single) quotes, so the parser's `Expr::Quoted` can't tell eval whether to interpolate.

Fix (cross-lane):
1. Lexer (msm-0's lane): carry the quote kind ‚Äî e.g. `Token::Quoted { content, interpolate: bool }` (true for `"‚Ä¶"`, false for `'‚Ä¶'`) or a distinct token ‚Äî and likewise `Expr::Quoted`/a new `Expr::Interpolated` in the parser.
2. Eval (aur-1): in eval, call `self.context.interpolate(&content)` for interpolating strings (I already have Context::interpolate); leave raw/bare literal.

Discovered by aur-1 while adding greedy eval-time read tests (bd-91e573). I can do the eval-side wiring once the lexer/parser carries the interpolate flag.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: [EPIC] Deterministic evaluator & operator realisation
description: SPEC ¬ßEvaluation model, ¬ßModes, ¬ßConfig operators. Bottom-up evaluator + the non-LLM realisation strategies so the whole `tests:` block passes offline. Child scope (label `eval`): DAG evaluator (operand-first); realisation resolution (`command`/`reduce`/`template`/`join`/`model`) + mode selection; template realisation (`%`, `%0`/`%1`, `%%`); join realisation (variadic); numeric `reduce` builtins (add/sub/mul/div/pow); command realisation (`NLIR_ARGS`, stdout); operand type coercion before apply; list rendering via `_sep` + spread into variadic ops; nullary-pop stack consumption.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Eval: wire Mode::Llm realisation to llm::realise_llm
description:
Wire eval::Evaluator::realise()'s Mode::Llm arm (src/eval.rs) to call aur-2's landed llm::realise_llm helper (bd-dc3c72), replacing the current EvalError::Unsupported placeholder. This is the eval side of the eval‚Üîllm seam; the llm.rs helper (resolve_model + substitute_operands + fragments + NLIR vars + backend dispatch + extract_result) is done.

Plan: in the Mode::Llm arm, if op has a prompt, render the (grouped-parens-preserved) operands to Vec<String>, call llm::realise_llm(op_cfg.model.as_deref(), prompt, &args, self.config, None /*cli_model*/, |n| std::env::var(n).ok()), wrap Ok(String) in Value::string, map the llm::RealiseError into a new EvalError::Llm(String). An llm op with no prompt stays Unsupported. Test via a command-type model (deterministic, no network); leave anthropic HTTP untested. Owned by aur-1 (eval lane), composes aur-2's bd-dc3c72.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: CI: self-hosted release workflow on v* tag push
description: Parent epic bd-e0a557 (label `release`). `.github/workflows/release.yml` on self-hosted runner(s), triggered by `v*` tags: build `nlir` binaries via the flake (linux x86_64/aarch64, darwin arm64 as applicable) plus `.sha256` sidecars, stream assets straight into a DRAFT GitHub release, then publish after an asset-inspection gate confirms the expected binary set (the stream-to-draft-then-inspect pattern). Mirror `omni-cli`/`tendril`/`kittui`/`ring-mods` `release.yml`; see the cacophony SPEC release section for the streaming/inspection rationale. Keep runners self-hosted with the correct labels.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Dedup negative-index-from-end resolver (stack + messages resolve_index)
description: `Stack::resolve_index` (src/stack.rs) and `messages::resolve_index` (src/messages.rs) are the identical negative-index-from-end resolver: `index >= 0` from the start, `index < 0` from the end (`-1` = last), `None` out of range. Both back SPEC index semantics (`$N` stack index with `$0` bottom / `$-1` top; `^N` message index). A shared helper (e.g. `fn resolve_index(len, i64) -> Option<usize>` in a small `index`/util module, or one made pub and reused) would dedup ~6 lines and keep the semantics in one place. Low priority; the two are currently in-sync. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-d4631b/bd-22fa7e session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: Parser+lexer: key=RHS assignment (Expr::Assign) + _-prefixed keys
description: SPEC ¬ßContext: read & assign. Parser: add Expr::Assign{key,value}; `key=RHS` where key is a literal identifier (Bare, incl `_`-prefixed system key), `=` is the builtin Equals token (lowest precedence, right-assoc), RHS is an expression. Yields the value; writes immediately (eval-side is aur-1's bd-c85dee). Lexer: allow `_`-prefixed bare keys so `_sep=`/`_cache=` lex. Unblocks aur-1 evaluator `=` handling + context bd-c85dee. Parser epic bd-ab25f7 was closed; this is a small follow-on in the parser/lexer lane.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: Project skeleton: Nix flake + Rust `nlir` CLI with crate deps wired
description:
Foundational bead. `SPEC.md` at the repo root is the normative contract for the whole project ‚Äî read it first; all beads implement it.

Set up the initial project scaffolding so every downstream bead has somewhere to land:

- **Nix flake** managing a Rust CLI, following the same pattern as the other harryaskham Rust CLIs. Use the **`omni-cli`** project as the primary reference for the flake + CLI structure (mature examples also live under `~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/‚Ä¶`, e.g. `tendril`, `kittui`).
- **Rust binary crate `nlir`** with the command-tree scaffolding and stub subcommands from SPEC ¬ßCLI surface: `-e`, `parse`, `test`, `repl`, `set`, `get`, `append-message`, `mcp stdio`, `self-update`, `feedback`, plus global flags (`--config`, `--context-file`, `--session-file`, `--mode`, `--model`, `--parallelism`, `--quiet`, `--dry-run`).
- **Wire crate dependencies on the friend-project template stack** (these are configured Cacophony friend projects / sub-crates):
  - `mcp-cli` ‚Äî gives the MCP server almost for free (`nlir mcp stdio`).
  - `updatable-cli` ‚Äî `nlir self-update`.
  - `feedback-cli` ‚Äî `nlir feedback`.
  Reference how `omni-cli` consumes these three crates and mirror that wiring.

Acceptance: `nix build`, `nix flake check`, and `cargo build` are green; `nlir --help` lists the command tree; the three template subcommands are wired (even if their handlers are thin) so later beads only fill in behaviour.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: fresh agent checkouts lack a git author identity for direct commits
description:
A managed agent checkout has no git `user.name`/`user.email` configured, so the
first direct `git commit` a dev worker makes fails with "Author identity
unknown ... Please tell me who you are" until the agent sets a local identity.
Prior mainline commits are authored `Cacophony Daemon <daemon@cacophony.local>`,
so workers must discover and replicate that convention by hand. Suggested fix:
seed a repo-local git identity (matching the daemon convention) at checkout
creation, or document the expected identity in AGENTS/SPEC. May be a
cacophony-harness concern rather than nlir-specific. Filed via reflect-session
mixin from bd-700306 session.

## Note (msm-0, 2026-07-03 ~19:55)

Current state: managed nlir checkouts now DO carry a repo-local git identity ‚Äî both my agent checkout and the daemon canonical checkouts (`~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/{nlir,cacophony}`) have `user.name`/`user.email` set locally, and a global `~/.gitconfig` identity also exists as a fallback. So direct `git commit` works today; this appears resolved at the harness level since the 16:27 filing. Leaving open as a harness/dx concern for aur-2 / the cacophony-harness owner to confirm the seeding is intentional + universal (fresh checkouts on a host without a global identity). Not an nlir-repo code change.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-2

title: config: env-interp clobbers bare $vars in command: bash blocks
description:
Config env-interpolation (bd-b342fd) expands a BARE `$name` inside `command:` bash blocks, clobbering shell variables that collide with a SET env var. Concretely: aur-1's `@`/echo command uses bash `$out`; the nix dev-shell exports `out` (nix build output path), so config interp rewrites `$out` -> that path and the command breaks. Passes in clean CI where `out` is unset, so it is an environment-dependent flake (surfaced via eval::tests::command_realisation_runs_under_bash).

Options to consider (SPEC decision needed): (a) config interp only expands `${VAR}` braces, not bare `$name` (but SPEC line ~330 uses bare `$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`); (b) skip interpolation entirely for `command:` values and let bash do its own expansion; (c) only expand names that are explicitly config-referenced, leaving unknown `$name` literal; (d) require `$$` escaping in command blocks. Recommend (b): do not env-interpolate `command:` blocks. Owner: config lane (msm-0). Low urgency (env-specific) but a real footgun for anyone whose shell exports a colliding var.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: [build] Linux x86 nix dev-shell coreutils segfaulting
description:
Reported by Harry (2026-07-03): on Linux x86, coreutils tools segfault inside the nlir `nix develop` dev shell, blocking `nix develop --command cargo build` for the Aurora agents.

Context / likely cause:
- nlir's flake pins nixpkgs to collective's exact rev (flakehub tarball rev 0590cd39) per operator request, to reuse the cached toolchain.
- The dev shell is only required on macOS to supply `libiconv` for the `-liconv` link (ureq/rustls TLS stack via feedback-cli/updatable-cli). On Linux, glibc already provides iconv.

WORKAROUND for Linux x86 (unblocks Aurora agents now):
- Build WITHOUT the dev shell: plain `cargo build` with a system Rust toolchain should link fine on Linux (no `-liconv` needed). Then `cargo fmt`/`clippy`/`test` directly.
- If a system Rust toolchain is unavailable, the nix rustc/cargo binaries themselves are fine; only coreutils in the interactive shell env is segfaulting ‚Äî invoke tools by absolute path or use `nix build .#nlir` for a one-shot binary.

Diagnostics needed (from a Linux x86 node):
1. Is this nlir-specific or do OTHER cacophony project dev shells (omni-cli, tendril, collective) also segfault coreutils on the same node? If all projects, it is environmental (node/store), not the nlir flake.
2. Which exact coreutils tool + command segfaults (e.g. `cp`, `mkdir`, `cat`)? `dmesg`/exit signal?
3. Does `nix store verify --repair /nix/store/...coreutils...` or re-fetch fix it (partial/corrupt download)?
4. Does omni-cli (which pins github nixos-unstable, a NEWER nixpkgs) work on the same node? If yes, the pinned rev 0590cd39's coreutils is the culprit on that hardware and we may need a devShell nixpkgs bump for Linux.

Filed from the nlir config-vertical session (msm-0). Not claimed: needs a Linux x86 repro to fix.

MITIGATION LANDED (Harry, c08b7a9): removed `use flake` from `.envrc` so direnv no longer auto-loads the nix dev shell on `cd`. Practical build workflow now:
- Linux x86: build with the SYSTEM Rust toolchain ‚Äî plain `cargo build` / `cargo test` / `cargo clippy` / `cargo fmt`. No dev shell needed (glibc provides iconv), which sidesteps the segfaulting dev-shell coreutils.
- macOS: still needs `libiconv`, so build via an explicit `nix develop --command cargo build` (the dev shell is fine on macOS; only Linux x86 coreutils segfaults).
Bead stays OPEN as a tracked known-issue: the underlying reason the pinned-rev dev-shell coreutils segfaults on Linux x86 is still undiagnosed and could resurface for `nix build`/CI. Diagnostics in the original description still wanted.

DIAGNOSTICS (aurora Linux x86_64, NixOS 6.18.32, nix 2.34.7; 2026-07-03, aur-2):

NOT REPRODUCIBLE on aurora now. Findings against the original questions:

1. nlir-specific vs environmental: NEITHER segfaults currently. nlir dev shell
   coreutils-9.8 (/nix/store/wkkwxc04gdw6b263l1h29pjarjnjdyb6-coreutils-9.8) AND
   omni-cli dev shell coreutils-9.11
   (/nix/store/9ypz3flqsrl5xl495mm8h645gadjsxi1-coreutils-9.11) both run
   cp/mkdir/cat/ls/mv/rm with exit=0.
2. Which tool segfaults: NONE currently -- cp/mkdir/cat/ls/mv/rm all exit=0 in
   the nlir dev shell.
3. store verify/repair: "nix store verify --no-trust <coreutils-9.8 path>"
   returns clean (exit 0) -- the path is intact, NOT corrupt now.
4. omni-cli (newer nixpkgs): works (coreutils-9.11). AND nlir's own pinned-rev
   coreutils-9.8 works too.

Also verified the original failing command path: "nix develop path:<nlir>
--command" runs cargo 1.91.0 + rustc 1.91.1 fine (TOOLCHAIN-OK).

CONCLUSION: The original segfault was almost certainly TRANSIENT store corruption
(a partial/corrupt download of the coreutils-9.8 store path) at report time,
since re-fetched/repaired -- the coreutils-9.8 path now verifies clean and works.
The pinned rev 0590cd39 is EXONERATED (a clean coreutils-9.8 works; no devShell
nixpkgs bump needed). The system-toolchain mitigation (c08b7a9) is the durable
Linux workflow and CI is green on it. Recommend resolving; reopen if the segfault
recurs (transient corruption can recur on a fresh partial download on any node).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: LLM: realise_llm helper (eval‚Üîllm realisation seam)
description:
Parent epic bd-b71b0b (label `llm`). Add a single high-level LLM-mode realisation helper in src/llm.rs so eval::realise()'s Mode::Llm arm (currently Unsupported) is a one-call wire on the eval.rs side (aur-1 owns eval; aur-2 owns this llm.rs helper ‚Äî agreed 2026-07-03).

Signature (confirmed with aur-1):
  realise_llm(model_alias: Option<&str>, prompt_template: &str, operands: &[String], config: &Config, cli_model: Option<&str>, env_lookup: impl Fn(&str)->Option<String>) -> Result<String, RealiseError>

Composes the landed pieces: resolve_model (op alias -> cli_model -> defaults.model precedence) + substitute_operands (fill %) + resolve_prompt_fragments + assemble_nlir_vars, then dispatch on model.kind ‚Äî anthropic via run_anthropic_backend (substitute_nlir_vars over messages), command via run_command_backend with the NLIR_ARGS bash-array declaration prepended so command operators can index ${NLIR_ARGS[k]}. Returns the raw extracted result String (caller wraps in Value::string). RealiseError { Model | Command | Anthropic } with Display+Error. Filed via the eval‚Üîllm coordination.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-2

title: Lexer: `_` echo operator can't tokenise (lex_bare consumes `_` mid-token)
description:
The SPEC `_` echo operator (op: "_", the det-echo test `xxx_2` ‚Üí "xxx xxx") cannot be tokenised. lex_bare (src/lexer.rs ~line 369) treats `_` as a bare-token continuation char (`c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_'`) ‚Äî a consequence of allowing `_`-prefixed system keys like `_sep`. So `xxx_2` lexes as a single Bare("xxx_2") and the configured `_` operator never splits it; the evaluator returns the input unchanged.

Repro: with a config operator `echo: { op: "_", arity: 2, fixity: infix, command: ... }`, `nlir -e 'xxx_2'` (det) yields "xxx_2" instead of "xxx xxx".

This blocks SPEC det-echo. The fix is in the lexer lane (msm-0): disambiguate the `_` echo operator from `_`-in-bare-token / `_`-prefixed keys ‚Äî e.g. only treat leading `_` as a bare/key start (not a mid-token continuation), or let a configured operator sigil `_` win when it appears between operands, while `_name` at a key position still lexes as a key. Command realisation itself (bd-3c1e6d) is verified working via a non-`_` sigil in src/eval.rs tests; only the `_`-sigil lexing is blocked. Filed by aur-1 from the eval SPEC-completion batch (bd-c85dee/bd-02a795/bd-3c1e6d).
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1
spoken_name: lexer underscore echo operator tokenization bug

Reference `omni-cli`/`tendril`/`ring-mods` self-update wiring (the `*_self_update_*` surfaces). Depends on the release-workflow bead (needs published assets to update onto).

title: CLI: context-source precedence
description:
Parent epic bd-bc848a (label `cli`). `--context-file` > `--session-file` > env > default file.

[note from aur-1, bd-0909ab context store landing] The precedence LOGIC now lives in `nlir::context::Context::load(LoadSources, &ContextConfig)` (src/context.rs). This CLI bead is the wiring layer: resolve the ambient env into a `LoadSources` and call `Context::load`. Semantics the store already implements (strict first-present-wins): `--context-file` > session import > `NLIR_CONTEXT` env > default file. Write-through targets: `--context-file` and the default file are file-backed; `NLIR_CONTEXT` env and session import are TRANSIENT (in-memory only, no write-through). Session-file *format parsing* is NOT this bead nor the store ‚Äî it belongs to the sessions epic (bd-720cdb parse ‚Üí Map, bd-000666 merge); that Map is handed to `LoadSources.session`. `default_context_path(&cfg, HOME)` + `expand_tilde` resolve `context.file_default` (tilde-expanded). SPEC clarity gap surfaced while implementing: the SPEC ¬ßCLI list writes "--session-file merge" which could read as merge-on-top-of-lower-precedence rather than strict-precedence; the store chose strict first-present-wins ‚Äî confirm that's the intended reading when wiring this.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

bead.title: Dedup negative-index-from-end resolver (stack + messages resolve_index)
bead.description: `Stack::resolve_index` (src/stack.rs) and `messages::resolve_index` (src/messages.rs) are the identical negative-index-from-end resolver: `index >= 0` from the start, `index < 0` from the end (`-1` = last), `None` out of range. Both back SPEC index semantics (`$N` stack index with `$0` bottom / `$-1` top; `^N` message index). A shared helper (e.g. `fn resolve_index(len, i64) -> Option<usize>` in a small `index`/util module, or one made pub and reused) would dedup ~6 lines and keep the semantics in one place. Low priority; the two are currently in-sync. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-d4631b/bd-22fa7e session.
bead.creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1
